item.title: 8 years ago you gave us a nudge. Thank you.
item.description: 8 years ago you gave us 16,000 upvotes and 483,000 views. That was an exciting day. Today, we hit 17 million downloaded files! We credit all of you dear imgurians for your kindness. It was the nudge to end all nudges. You people are the best! -Randy and Deanna (The OlderGeeks) Edit: Holy smokes! Again? Thanks again, nice people. Send clicks to our website and find something for yourself! https://www.oldergeeks.com
item.dc:creator: oldergeeks

item.dc:creator: PiercedViking

item.title: I don't know what to do with myself, feels surreal.
item.description: I started at my current job in February of 2022 as a temp at $20/hr. After 6 months, I was officially hired on by the company, got a $0.25 bump. Big money. I was told I had just missed the cutoff for their annual reviews and that I'd get one the following year. I've been told I'm doing a great job, I rarely ever see my boss and he rarely needs to check in on me. I'm praised by ither departments. My boss has gotten nervous when I expressed interest in other departments and even downright talked badly about one. It's clear he likes me right where I am. I got a whopping 98 cents at my review, barely over inflation. I started looking for jobs that night. I had an interview yesterday at a union warehouse and it went well. They called me a few hours later with an offer: $25.69/hr plus an additional $1.70 for shift differential (second shift). With the union pay scale, I'll be at $32 in just a few years. I'm beside myself. Unions work, don't let anyone tell you otherwise.

item.title: Joseph Medicine Crow was a bad ass
item.description: Some sauce: https://allthatsinteresting.com/joe-medicine-crow 'Becoming a war chief was not easy — and even less so after the U.S. government had all but eradicated the Indigenous way of life on the Great Plains. The feat required passing four tests, including touching a living enemy, stealing an enemy’s weapon, and successfully leading a war party. Finally, a person had to sneak into an enemy camp to steal a horse. Generations of men passed this test and became war chiefs. But the last feat became all but impossible as Native Americans were forced onto reservations and horses slowly disappeared from the battlefield in the 20th century. And yet Joe Medicine Crow managed to accomplish all four tasks almost by accident, and in a place few members of the Crow Nation had ever visited. He earned the honor fighting Nazis in Germany with the U.S. Army.' To help answer the horse questions: https://www.smithsonianmag.com/smart-news/native-americans-spread-horses-through-the-west-earlier-than-thought-180981912/ Dog pulled Travois: http://www.native-languages.org/travois.htm
item.dc:creator: HorrorMango

item.title: Just a random dump of the stupid things I laugh at
item.description:
1
2
3
4
5
6
7
8
9. The man's got talent, can't deny it
10
11
12. Blake Lively's husband at it again
13
14. And finally I want to thank the 2 (Hi, Mom!) people that made it to end of the dump. I wanted to share with you that as well as browsing and posting on Imgur all day I'm also making a passion project of mine. If you liked the humor in this dump I think you will like the game too. It is inspired by "The Stanley Parable" and similar in tone and features funny signs like this one here. Can you imagine? A first person game mixed with humor? https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Anyway, It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked the game out on Steam and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just do not press the big red button, tho.

item.title: Memes vol 250
item.description:
Have you SEEN my room?! One of you said it looks like my personality exploded on the walls and that’s the best description I’ve ever heard
My doctor told me I had a “toddler diet” (it’s true) and I’m always the last 2
I feel like I could post my room to Pinterest and have minor success- like it’s just batshit insanity up in here
I found this the day after I went to the beach with my friends. But we made a worm on a string out of sand so it was a win win
I say “what” without the question mark
This is my background. Billy is not having a great day :(
This might be the best thing I’ve ever read (and I’ve read Moon Knight by Lemire and Smallwood)
I read the cross one to my overly religious father and he got mad?? He was acting like it was blasphemy
Coricopat and Tantomile
Look at that face- he’s doing it to spite you I just know it
Once
You guys I don’t think I’m gonna marry Robert Irwin. That’s literally my only dream in life but he lives in Australia sooo (and he already has a girlfriend) Bindi the jungle girl was one of my favorite shows as a kid and I love their family. Also Bindi and Chandler’s wedding photos are ELITE!! The giraffes?! The snakes?!
This is so in character for both of them. Ted and Linda from Starkid musicals- Black Friday and The Guy Who Didn’t Like Musicals
You know that wolf turned some kids into furries
Oops sorry for not cropping
She was terrifying- slay Skye you killed it
He literally does this in 1992sies
Gabriel Picolo my beloved
Froggy and Liza Jane tax
Gidget tax (and Schroeder)
Coricopat tax- or Tantomile I can’t tell
Scruffy tax
Nessie tax

item.title: Toby Keith (1961 - 2024)
item.description:
It's hard forget the controversy that went down surrounding The Chicks (formerly the Dixie Chicks) in 2003 that resulted in the blacklisting of their music. This controversy was also tied into a feud between The Chicks and fellow Nashville country singer Toby Keith. Here's a breakdown of what happened between the two artists.
Before The Chicks' lead singer Natalie Maines shared her opinion about the Iraq War and the President of the United States, which resulted in their music being banned from country radio stations across the country, The Maines/Keith feud was sparked by a comment the singer made about Keith's 2002 song, "Courtesy of the Red, White and Blue (The Angry American)." The song was written and released after the death of Keith's veteran father and the September 11, 2001 attacks. The song is unapologetic, with Keith essentially singing his opinion about the actions the US should take, most notably singing, "We'll put a boot in your a— it's the American way." Maines shared her opinion about the song and Keith as a songwriter with the Los Angeles Times in 2002.
"I hate it," she said at the time. "It's ignorant, and it makes country music sound ignorant. It targets an entire culture - and not just the bad people who did bad things. You've got to have some tact. Anybody can write, 'We'll put a boot in your a—.' But a lot of people agree with it. The kinds of songs I prefer on the subject are like Bruce Springsteen's new songs." Not long after that comment, Maines shared her opinion on the invasion of Iraq and President George W. Bush during a tour stop in London, England in March 2003. "Just so you know, we're on the good side with y'all. We do not want this war, this violence, and we're ashamed that the President of the United States is from Texas," she told the crowd. This comment led to massive backlash in the United States, with country radio stations banning their music and the group taking a step back from the spotlight.
After Maines' comments about his song and the war, Keith kept their feud going by displaying a fake photo of Maines next to Saddam Hussein at his concerts. Maines then fired back at Keith by wearing a t-shirt with the letters "F.U.T.K." onstage at the Academy of Country Music Awards, which many saw as a jab at Keith. At the time, the trio said the letters stood for "Friends United in Truth and Kindness," but Maines later confirmed in the trio's documentary, Shut Up And Sing, that the shirt did mean what most people thought, according to Taste of Country.
Like many things, the Natalie Maines/Toby Keith feud eventually fizzled out, with Keith announcing he was done with it after watching a friend go through his child's cancer battle. "I saw a picture on the cover of Country Weekly with a picture of me and Natalie and it said, 'Fight to the Death' or something. It seemed so insignificant. I said, 'Enough is enough,'" he said in 2003. After taking a break, the Chicks came back in 2006 with their single "Not Ready To Make Nice" from their Taking The Long Way album, which addressed the backlash they received after Maines shared her opinion, but did not directly address the controversy with Keith. The song peaked at number four on the Billboard Hot 100 and went on to win three Grammy Awards.
Long Live The Chicks.
item.dc:creator: telecomteardown

item.title: One District at a Time: Tennessee law denied Allie Phillips an abortion. So she's now running for office
item.description: The path to Tennessee politics for Allie Phillips began last year in her doctor's office. She was 19 weeks pregnant when she got the devastating news about her unborn daughter: only two of the four chambers in her heart were formed. It was one of many severe congenital issues. The fetus was incompatible with life. Phillips is 28. She and her husband already have a 6-year-old daughter. Phillips and her husband live a modest life. Phillips runs a daycare out of her house, and her husband is a forklift mechanic. Flying out of state on a few days' notice wasn't something they could do with ease, so they started a fundraiser and asked friends and family for help. After days of frantic phone calls around the country, she made an appointment at a clinic in New York to have the procedure. When she got there, the fetal heartbeat had already stopped. She was in danger of becoming septic. "I'm very thankful for that clinic because they treated me like a human being," Phillips says. "Unlike my state did." "The fight for breaking the super majority is gonna run through the suburbs," says Uffelman, tracing his finger along the Montgomery County line. Tennessee is one of nearly 20 states that have a Republican supermajority, with large majorities in both legislative chambers and control of the governor's office. Breaking that supermajority – that's what victory would look like for Tennesse Democrats. Phillips' district is one that Democrats have identified as flippable. Running for office, she says, is her way of fighting for that safety — for her daughter and everyone else's daughters. https://www.npr.org/2024/02/05/1228326177/abortion-allie-phillips-tennessee-reproductive-rights-pregnancy
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.dc:creator: ComradeBinch

item.title: Should be easy
item.description: Most Viral edit: A lot of comments saying, "they aren't willing to learn" so I'd like to speak to that. We're falling for the classic "us vs. them" trope. Sure, there are Trump supporters who can't be reached. But among them there are many less vocal people who are just going with the flow. They are likely "playing the part" but are swayable, if you addresses issues that are central to their lives. If we speak to their needs from a place of honesty and integrity, we will win many of them to our side. Then we have an opportunity to politically educate them. Show how being progressive can help them AND the people they were taught to hate. It's a lifelong struggle, but one that we have to engage in to make the world a safer place for vulnerable communities. https://www.google.com/search?q=More+Than+Half+of+Americans+Read+Below+a+6th-Grade+Level

item.dc:creator: janeQdoe

item.title: Dammit, Jim, I'm a draftsman, not a doctor.
item.description: Just yelling into the void here everyone, avoid the wall of text, best to just move on to funnier memes. My other half developed a problem swallowing food. Food would get lodged in her throat easily, and it would be very hard to get that blockage to move up or down. When she sought out medical advice for this, her doctor apparently went to WebMD and picked whatever needed the most expensive testing and treatment while also fitting her symptoms, and he decided she had something called "chiari malformation". This is where part of the brain protrudes from the bottom of the skull, where the brain stem is. Various scans and tests said she had it but just barely. Borderline case, she shouldn't even notice. But to "fix" the issue, where she could eat normally, would require surgery to open up the hole in the base of her skull, and it probably wouldn't help. Late last year, I tried to research (I DiD mY oWn ReSeArCh!) what other things could cause difficulty swallowing, because jumping right to a condition neither of us had ever heard of without checking anything else was something *I* was having trouble swallowing. It's like saying your car needs a whole new engine because of a misfire, sure that *could* fix it, bit there are more likely causes. Just need to do some diagnosis. And one thing that I found that was easy to test by experiment was allergies. So for two weeks, she tried taking a benadryl half an hour before eating. And it worked. No issues. She can just eat normally. We tried a "control" where she didn't take a benadryl all week, and food started getting lodged in her throat again. The issue went away when she tried the benadryl again. So now we need to figure out what she is allergic to, but that's a much better outlook than "Just have to live with it". And I have to ask, why didn't her doc think of this? What the fuck are his years of education and experience good for if he doesn't try the obvious and the problem is solved by a fucking high-school dropout and his wife? Dear old Dad told me a story about a lighthouse that makes me look for cheap and obvious fixes first, I guess this doctor didn't have parents with that sort of wisdom? What the fuck are we paying so much for?
item.dc:creator: IdiotSavantTinker

item.title: How's it Going, Capitalism? I got bombarded by these. I need more.
item.description:
Someone sent me this bunch of these memes and I love them. And yes, I know, broad over generalisations and some out of context articles blah blah. Let me have my fuuuuun.
This one is referring to tech that's less getting people to work and more letting folk make use of the out-of-the-box absurd creativity of dreams, from what I can figure out. (Though still for the purpose of creating solutions, I assume are work related?)
I don't have any photos of a pet to add tax of, sorry :c

item.title: Cake Day Life Update
item.description:
Many years ago (10.5) my boyfriend of 6 months handmade me a 3D Settlers of Catan game. The post hit front page and many of you encouraged me to husband him. Well Imgur suggested I post an update for my cake day so.....
I took your advice and we were married in Ireland in 2018! We still love to play tabletop games and travel as much as possible.
He's the best husband one could ask for and is about to take our two pups, Rogue and Remy, on a cross country trip as we relocate from west coast to east coast. I've shared so many things from Imgur with him over the years he's become an official lurker. Thanks for all the entertainment and the great advice :P Front page edit: Thanks for all the kind comments! We are super nerds so feel free to send us your favorite tabletop game suggestions. We're also going to be new to the Raleigh area and would love good food recommendations!
item.dc:creator: Ghambit
